_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to solve problems involving the surface area and volume of pyramids, cones, spheres and composite objects.

_source.w: We are learning to solve problems involving the surface area and volume of pyramids, cones, spheres and composite objects.
y: Extending volume and surface area calculations to curved and pointed shapes completes our toolkit for working with all common 3D objects.
These formulas are essential for engineering, architecture, manufacturing and any design work involving 3D objects.
Understanding these relationships develops our spatial reasoning and our ability to work with π in applied contexts.
eg: Calculating the volume of a cone: V = ⅓πr²h.
Finding the surface area of a sphere: SA = 4πr².
Solving: 'How much ice cream can a cone with radius 3 cm and height 12 cm hold?'
Calculating the volume of a composite object: a hemisphere on top of a cylinder.
Finding the surface area of a pyramid with a square base.
Comparing the volumes of a cone, sphere and cylinder with the same radius and height.
